lit: And Israel is sealed,
sm: And Israel is sealed,

syn.mc: Main clause in the temporal sequence begun at line 97.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ Ἰσραήλ. Verb: σφραγίζεται.
syn.rhet: The sealing of Israel answers the slaughtering of the lamb.

src: τότε ἀφίκετο ὁ ἄγγελος πατάσσειν Δίγυπτον,
ana: τότε ἀφίκετο ὁ ἄγγελος πατάσσειν Αἴγυπτον,
lit: Then the angel arrived to strike Egypt,
sm: Then the angel arrived to strike Egypt,

syn.mc: Main clause opening the consequence of the temporal protasis.
syn.sub: πατάσσειν is an infinitival complement expressing purpose or intended action.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ ἄγγελος. Verb: ἀφίκετο. Infinitive: πατάσσειν. Object of infinitive: Αἴγυπτον.
syn.rhet: The judgment scene now shifts from the lamb to the avenging angel.

lit: the one uninitiated in the mystery,
sm: the one not initiated into the mystery,

syn.mc: Appositive accusative phrase dependent on Αἴγυπτον.
syn.svo: Object-apposition to Αἴγυπτον: τὴν ἀμύητον τοῦ μυστηρίου.
syn.rhet: First of a chain of accusative appositives defining Egypt by lack.

lit: the one lacking the Passover,
sm: the one deprived of the Passover,

syn.mc: Appositive accusative phrase continuing the description of Egypt.
syn.svo: Object-apposition to Αἴγυπτον.
syn.rhet: The lack is named ritually: without Passover.

lit: the one unsealed by the blood,
sm: the one unsealed from the blood,

syn.mc: Appositive accusative phrase continuing the stack of epithets.
syn.svo: Object-apposition to Αἴγυπτον.
syn.rhet: The lack is now expressed in sacramental terms: no seal of blood.

lit: the one unguarded by the spirit,
sm: the one left unguarded by the spirit,

syn.mc: Appositive accusative phrase continuing the same series.
syn.svo: Object-apposition to Αἴγυπτον.
syn.rhet: The series moves from mystery to blood to spirit.

lit: the hostile one,
sm: the hostile one,

syn.mc: Final accusative epithet in the list.
syn.svo: Object-apposition to Αἴγυπτον.
syn.rhet: The list turns from ritual lack to active enmity.

lit: the faithless one,
sm: the faithless one,
emd: Restored accent on ἄπιστον and normalized the trailing OCR quotation mark as a comma.

syn.mc: Final appositive epithet before the main finite clause resumes.
syn.svo: Object-apposition to Αἴγυπτον.
syn.rhet: The list culminates in unbelief/faithlessness.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: Having struck in one night, he bereaved [them].
sm: After striking in one night, he bereaved them of children.

syn.mc: Main finite clause concluding the judgment sentence.
syn.sub: The aorist participle πατάξας is antecedent to the finite verb.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: ὁ ἄγγελος. Participle: πατάξας. Verb: ἠτέκνωσεν. Time phrase: ἐν μιᾷ νυκτί.
syn.rhet: The one-night devastation closes the appositive string with a decisive act.

ana: περιελθὼν γὰρ τὸν Ἰσραὴλ ὁ ἄγγελος
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: For the angel, having gone around Israel
sm: For the angel, having gone around Israel,
emd: Removed the stray initial breathing mark before περιελθών and normalized ᾿Ισραὴλ.

syn.mc: Participial phrase leading into the next action.
syn.sub: γάρ provides explanation for the preceding judgment.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ ἄγγελος. Participle: περιελθὼν. Object: τὸν Ἰσραήλ.
syn.ptc: περιελθὼν is circumstantial, explaining how the angel distinguished Israel from Egypt.
syn.rhet: The angel's circuit around Israel underscores protection rather than devastation.

src: καὶ ἰδὼν ἐσφραγισμένον τῷ τοῦ προβάτου αἵματι,
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
pf mp ptcp acc sg m
lit: and seeing [Israel] sealed with the blood of the sheep,
sm: and seeing [Israel] sealed with the blood of the sheep,

syn.mc: Participial phrase coordinated with line 110.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; object of ἰδών is understood from context.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: ὁ ἄγγελος. Participles: ἰδών, ἐσφραγισμένον. Dative phrase: τῷ τοῦ προβάτου αἵματι.
syn.ptc: The participles are circumstantial: first the angel surveys, then recognizes the sealed sign.
syn.rhet: Sight of the seal becomes the criterion for exemption.

lit: He came against Egypt,
sm: He came against Egypt,

syn.mc: Main clause continuing the angel's action.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ ἄγγελος. Verb: ἦλθεν. Prepositional phrase: ἐπ᾽ Αἴγυπτον.
syn.rhet: The movement from seeing the seal to striking Egypt is sharply marked.

src: καὶ τὸν σκληροτράχηλον Φαραὼ διὰ πένθους ἐθάμασεν,
ana: καὶ τὸν σκληροτράχηλον Φαραὼ διὰ πένθους ἐδάμασεν,
lit: and subdued the stiff-necked Pharaoh through mourning,
sm: and subdued the stiff-necked Pharaoh by mourning,

syn.mc: Main clause with object and instrumental phrase.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ ἄγγελος. Verb: ἐδάμασεν. Object: τὸν σκληροτράχηλον Φαραώ. Means: διὰ πένθους.
syn.rhet: Mourning is paradoxically the instrument of subduing Pharaoh.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: having clothed him not with a gray robe
sm: clothing him not with a gray robe,

syn.mc: Participial phrase elaborating the result of the angelic action.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the participle depends on the preceding narrative.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ ἄγγελος. Participle: ἐνδύσας. Object: αὐτόν. Negated object phrase: οὐ στολὴν φαιὰν.
syn.ptc: ἐνδύσας introduces the visual metaphor of clothing Pharaoh with mourning.
syn.rhet: The description shifts from blow to dress, making grief visible.

tok: pf mp ptcp acc sg m
lit: nor a torn veil,
sm: nor with a torn veil,

syn.mc: Coordinate negated object phrase continuing line 115.
syn.svo: Continuation of the object of ἐνδύσας: οὐδὲ πέπλον περιεσχισμένον.
syn.rhet: Second negated garment deepens the funerary metaphor.

tok: pf mp ptcp acc sg f
lit: but all Egypt torn and rent
sm: but rather all Egypt torn and rent,

syn.mc: Adversative correction to the previous negated garments.
syn.svo: Object phrase of ἐνδύσας continued: ὅλην Αἴγυπτον περιεσχισμένην.
syn.rhet: The garment metaphor expands from Pharaoh to the whole land.

tok: pres act ptcp acc sg f
lit: mourning over her firstborn.
sm: mourning over her firstborn.

syn.mc: Participial modifier completing the descriptive object phrase.
syn.svo: Implicit object: Αἴγυπτον. Participle: πενθοῦσαν. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τοῖς πρωτοτόκοις αὐτῆς.
syn.rhet: The loss of the firstborn is the emotional core of the scene.

src: ὅλη γὰρ Αἴγυπτος γενηθεῖσα ἐν πόνοις καὶ πληγαῖς,
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg f
lit: For all Egypt, having become in pains and blows,
sm: For all Egypt, having been brought into pains and blows,

syn.mc: Main descriptive clause introducing the next sentence.
syn.sub: The participle γενηθεῖσα forms a circumstantial characterization.
syn.svo: Subject: Αἴγυπτος. Verb: implied in the participle. Predicate adjective: ὅλη. Locative phrase: ἐν πόνοις καὶ πληγαῖς.
syn.rhet: The whole land is framed as a body in pain.

lit: in tears and lamentations,
sm: in tears and lamentations,

syn.mc: Prepositional phrase continuing line 119.
syn.svo: Dependent phrase modifying the state of Egypt.
syn.rhet: The paired nouns intensify the lamentation motif.

src: ἀφίκετο πρὸς τὸν Φαραὼ ὅλη πενθήρης
lit: It came to Pharaoh wholly in mourning,
sm: It came to Pharaoh wholly in mourning,

syn.mc: Main clause resuming the finite syntax after the participial description.
syn.svo: Subject: Αἴγυπτος. Verb: ἀφίκετο. Goal phrase: πρὸς τὸν Φαραώ. Predicative adjectives: ὅλη πενθήρης.
syn.rhet: The city or land is personified as a mourner approaching Pharaoh.

src: οὐ μόνον τῷ σχήματι ἀλλὰ καὶ τῇ ψυχῆ,
ana: οὐ μόνον τῷ σχήματι ἀλλὰ καὶ τῇ ψυχῇ,
lit: not only in appearance but also in soul,
sm: not only in appearance but also in soul,

syn.mc: Prepositional/dative phrase modifying the preceding subject.
syn.svo: Datives of respect: τῷ σχήματι, τῇ ψυχῇ.
syn.rhet: The suffering is both outward and inward.

src: περιεσχισμένη οὐ μόνον τὰς στολὰς τῆς περιβολῆς
tok: pf mp ptcp nom sg f
lit: torn not only the garments of the dress
sm: torn not only in the garments of her dress

syn.mc: Participial phrase elaborating the prior dative phrase.
syn.svo: Subject: Αἴγυπτος. Participle: περιεσχισμένη. Negated object phrase: οὐ μόνον τὰς στολὰς τῆς περιβολῆς.
syn.rhet: The outer dress is named before the inner loss in line 124.

src: ἀλλὰ καὶ τοὺς μασθοὺς τῆς τρυφῆς.
lit: but also the breasts of luxury.
sm: but also in the breasts of luxury.

syn.mc: Adversative continuation of the οὐ μόνον ... ἀλλὰ καὶ pattern.
syn.svo: Continuation of the object phrase under the participle of line 123: τοὺς μασθοὺς τῆς τρυφῆς.
syn.rhet: The inward grief reaches even the bodily core of pleasure.

lit: And it was a new sight to see,
sm: And it was a new sight to see,

syn.mc: Impersonal main clause introducing a vivid visual description.
syn.svo: Subject: καινὸν θέαμα. Verb: ἦν. Complement: ἰδεῖν.
syn.rhet: The narrative pauses to frame the spectacle explicitly.

tok: pres mp ptcp acc pl m
pres act ptcp acc pl m
lit: here lamenting, there wailing,
sm: here beating their breasts, there wailing,

syn.mc: Correlative descriptive phrase dependent on line 125.
syn.svo: Substantival participles function as the objects of sight.
syn.ptc: κοπτομένους and κωκύοντας are descriptive participles in antiphonal balance.
syn.rhet: ἔνθα ... ἔνθα gives the scene a theatrical alternation.

lit: and in the middle, Pharaoh in mourning
sm: and in the midst, Pharaoh in mourning,

syn.mc: Compressed descriptive phrase continuing the spectacle.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the attachment of the adjectives is stylistically condensed. <UNCERTAIN: the phrase is syntactically compressed, but the reading itself is clear.>
syn.svo: Prepositional/adverbial phrase: μέσον Φαραώ. Predicate adjective: πενθήρη.
syn.rhet: The scene centers on Pharaoh as the axis of mourning.

tok: pres mp ptcp acc sg m/n
lit: sitting on sackcloth and ashes,
sm: sitting on sackcloth and ashes,

syn.mc: Participial phrase dependent on the same descriptive scene.
syn.svo: Implicit referent: the central figure in the scene, likely Pharaoh or the angelic agent. Participle: καθήμενον. Locative phrase: ἐπὶ σάκκῳ καὶ σπόδῳ.
syn.ptc: The participle extends the visual tableau.
syn.rhet: The humiliation is staged through bodily posture.

src: περιβεβλημένον τὸ ψηλαφητὸν σκότος ὡς ἱμάτιον πενθικόν,
tok: pf mp ptcp acc sg n
lit: clothed with palpable darkness as a mourning garment,
sm: clothed with palpable darkness as a mourning garment,

syn.mc: Participial phrase continuing the same description.
syn.svo: Implicit referent continues from line 128. Participle: περιβεβλημένον. Object: τὸ ψηλαφητὸν σκότος. Comparative phrase: ὡς ἱμάτιον πενθικόν.
syn.rhet: The darkness becomes a visible garment of mourning.

src: περιεζωσμένον ὅλ[ην] Αἴγυπτον ὡς κιθῶνα πένθους.
ana: περιεζωσμένον ὅλ[ην] Αἴγυπτον ὡς χιτῶνα πένθους.
tok: pf mp ptcp acc sg n
lit: girded with all Egypt as with a tunic of mourning.
sm: girded with all Egypt as with a tunic of mourning.

syn.mc: Final participial image in the descriptive sequence.
syn.svo: Continuing participial referent from lines 127-129. Participle: περιεζωσμένον. Object: ὅλην Αἴγυπτον. Comparative phrase: ὡς χιτῶνα πένθους.
syn.rhet: The angel or central figure is figured as wrapped and girded by Egypt itself, completing the mourning tableau.
